# function: hmac

Returns the [HMAC-`algorithm`](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2104) of `message` keyed by `key`, hex-encoded.

`algorithm` is one of:

- `"sha1"` — RFC 2104 / FIPS 180-4 (legacy)
- `"sha224"`, `"sha256"`, `"sha384"`, `"sha512"` — FIPS 180-4 SHA-2 family
- `"sha512_224"`, `"sha512_256"` — truncated SHA-512 variants

`key` and `message` are interpreted as raw byte strings. If your key is hex- or base64-encoded, decode first (`base64decode(key)` etc.).

```
hmac("sha256", "super-secret", "payload")
→ "3da88…" (hex)
```

This function is a derivation, not an MAC verifier — produce the expected MAC and `==`-compare in HCL.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// HMAC (RFC 2104) — keyed message authentication code, hex-encoded. Useful at the seam where Terraform-rendered values feed a service that expects a signed request body.
output "webhook_signature" {
  value = provider::burnham::hmac("sha256", "shared-secret", "payload-bytes")
}

// SHA-512 for higher-security paths.
output "long_signature" {
  value = provider::burnham::hmac("sha512", "k", "m")
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
hmac(algorithm string, key string, message string) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `algorithm` (String) Hash algorithm: "sha1", "sha224", "sha256", "sha384", "sha512", "sha512_224", or "sha512_256".
1. `key` (String) The HMAC key, as raw bytes.
1. `message` (String) The message to authenticate.
